subject: Cloud Computing
title: Data Pipelines and ETL
description: A fast breakdown of Data Pipelines and ETL for builders.

hook.text: Raw data is messy. It arrives in different formats from dozens of sources. Before analysis, it needs cleaning.

buildup.text: ETL — Extract, Transform, Load — pulls data from sources, cleans and reshapes it, then loads it into a warehouse.

discovery.text: Cloud pipelines like AWS Glue, Azure Data Factory, and Dataflow automate ETL at scale.

twist.text: ELT is the new trend: load raw data first, transform later. Cloud warehouses are powerful enough to handle it.

climax.text: A reliable pipeline is the backbone of data-driven decisions. Garbage in, garbage out — pipelines ensure quality.

punchline.text: Clean data in, good decisions out. Pipelines make that happen.

quiz.question: What is the difference between ETL and ELT?
options: ETL transforms data before loading; ELT loads raw data first and transforms later
ETL is for cloud; ELT is for on-premises only
ETL processes data faster than ELT in all cases
